slides.title: SvelteKit & Elysia Mastery
slides.subtitle:
The Ultimate Full-Stack Developer Experience
From Reactive Runes to Type-Safe Elysia APIs

slides.name: Aravind Govindhasamy
slides.title: Full-Stack Developer
slides.experience: Specialist in Reactive Frameworks & High-Performance APIs
slides.oss_experience: Open Source Contributor & Core Contributor to Reactive Tooling

box.content: The Modern Meta-framework.
SvelteKit uses a directory-based router where folders define routes and files like +page.svelte define content.

list.text: Predictable: Structure clearly maps to URLs.
Dynamic: Folders with [brackets] handle dynamic parameters.

box.content: Fetch data before the page renders, supporting both server-side and client-side execution.
The standard way to handle form submissions without manual fetch calls, using native HTML forms with progressive enhancement.
SvelteKit is runtime-agnostic through its adapter system (Vercel, Netlify, Cloudflare, Node).

list.text: Flexibility: Build once, deploy to any cloud provider.
Edge: Optimized for edge runtimes with adapter-cloudflare.

box.content: Mastering Svelte 5 Runes.
Runes are a set of primitives that provide universal reactivity, replacing top-level variables and stores in many cases.

list.text: Signal-based: Efficient fine-grained updates.
Simplified: Use reactivity anywhere, not just in .svelte files.

box.content: Declares reactive state in a way that is modern, explicit, and highly performant.
box.code:
let count = $state(0);
const increment = () => count += 1;

box.content: Computed values that automatically update when their dependencies change.
box.code:
let count = $state(0);
let doubled = $derived(count * 2);

box.content: Handles side effects, replacing the old $: syntax with more control and better performance.
The new way to define component props with full TypeScript support and cleaner syntax.
box.code: let { name, age } = $props();

box.content: The High-Performance API Machine.
A high-performance Bun-native framework focusing on developer experience and type safety.

list.text: Fast: Outperforms Express and Fastify in Bun.
Safe: Built-in end-to-end type safety.

box.content: Elysia uses static analysis to optimize route execution and generate OpenAPI schemas automatically.

list.text: Efficient: Predictable performance at scale.
Swagger: Instant documentation with easy-to-use plugins.

box.content: A unified object containing request, response, set (headers/status), and more.
Elysia leverages Bun's native features like `Bun.file` and `Bun.serve` for maximum throughput.
box.code: app.get('/video', () => Bun.file('movie.mp4'));

box.content: End-to-End Type Safety Simplified.
A high-performance client for Elysia that provides full type safety without code generation or heavy dependencies.

list.text: Seamless: Shares types directly from your Elysia server.
Zero-Config: No need for tRPC-style boilerplate.

box.content: The standard client that allows you to call your API using object property access, with full IDE autocompletion.
A lightweight fetch wrapper that provides type safety for standard fetch-like calls.
box.code:
const fetch = edenFetch<App>('http://localhost:3000');
const data = await fetch('/user/:id', { params: { id: '1' } });

box.content: Eden automatically extracts validation schemas (Zod, TypeBox) and reflects them in the client types.

list.text: Reliable: If the schema changes, the client type changes instantly.
Safety: Prevents sending invalid data to the server.

box.content: Bridging SvelteKit and Elysia.
Strategy for combining SvelteKit's SSR with Elysia's high-speed API logic.

list.text: SSR Loaders: Fetch initial data from Elysia during server-side render.
Hydration: Eden client takes over for interactive client-side updates.

box.content: Best practices for proxying SvelteKit form actions to an Elysia backend for specialized processing.
Running both SvelteKit and Elysia on the edge (e.g., Cloudflare Workers) for global performance.

list.text: Low Latency: Compute happens near the user.
Unified Runtime: Shared Bun environment simplifies configuration.

box.content: Using Eden for reactive WebSocket or SSE connections that update Svelte state in real-time.
Elysia's Modular Plugin System.
Elysia follows a 'composable' plugin model where any instance can be used as a plugin for another instance.
box.code:
const plugin = new Elysia().get('/p', () => 'Ping');
const app = new Elysia().use(plugin);

box.content: Hook into request execution at various stages: `onRequest`, `beforeHandle`, `afterHandle`, `onResponse`, and `onError`.
Elysia provides a rich set of official plugins for common tasks.

list.text: Swagger: Interactive API documentation.
JWT: Token-based authentication.
CORS: Cross-Origin Resource Sharing.

box.content: Creating reusable plugins is easy with the `.use()` method and type inference.
box.code:
const auth = (app) => app.onBeforeHandle(...) 
app.use(auth);

box.content: Managing Data in SvelteKit.
A look at the traditional `writable`, `readable`, and `derived` stores for global state management.
box.code:
import { writable } from 'svelte/store';
export const theme = writable('light');

box.content: How Runes simplify global state and cross-component communication without explicit stores.

list.text: Intuitive: Regular classes can now be reactive.
Decoupled: Better separation of concerns.

box.content: Implementing smooth UX by updating the UI before the server responds, using SvelteKit's `enhance` action.
box.code:
<form use:enhance={(...) => {
  // Preliminary state update
  return async ({ update }) => { ... };
}}>

box.content: Connecting Elysia's real-time events to Svelte's reactive variables for instant feedback loops.

list.text: Socket to Rune: Direct mapping of event data to $state.
Feedback: User actions reflected immediately across all clients.

box.content: Custom Preprocessors & Elysia Macros.
Customizing the compilation step. How to use tools like `svelte-preprocess` or `vite-plugin-svelte` for specialized syntax.

list.text: PostCSS/SCSS: Advanced styling techniques.
Custom Logic: Injecting code or transforming markup at build time.

box.content: Define custom route properties that can inject logic and metadata automatically, improving code reuse and safety.
Pushing TypeScript to the limit. Inferring complex nested structures and union types across the full-stack boundary.

list.text: Deep Types: Navigating complex API responses with ease.
Strictness: Catching subtle type mismatches during development.

box.content: Extending Svelte's capabilities with custom `use:action` directives for DOM manipulation or third-party library integration.
box.code: Hover me

box.content: Drizzle ORM & Turso Integration.
The standard choice for Bun-first apps: Type-safe, lightweight, and focused on predictable SQL performance.
A distributed SQLite database built on LibSQL, perfect for low-latency global applications.

list.text: Global Replication: Data stays close to your compute.
Scale: Handle millions of databases with ease.

box.content: Using Drizzle Kit to generate and push migrations, staying in sync with your TypeScript schemas.
box.code:
# shell
bun drizzle-kit generate:sqlite

box.content: Connecting database updates to Svelte state. Using SvelteKit's `invalidate` or `invalidateAll` to refresh data from Elysia.
Multi-runtime Flexibility.
Running the entire stack on a single Bun instance for maximum simplicity and performance.
box.code: bun index.ts // Servant both SvelteKit and Elysia

box.content: Leveraging Cloudflare's edge infrastructure. Elysia handles the backend logic while SvelteKit manages the frontend.

list.text: Pages: Host the SvelteKit frontend.
Workers: Run the Elysia API.

box.content: Creating a lightweight Bun-based Docker image for consistent deployment across any cloud provider.
box.code:
FROM oven/bun:latest
COPY . .
CMD ["bun", "src/index.ts"]

box.content: Hosting the frontend on a CDN (Vercel/Netlify) and the Elysia API on a dedicated VPS or serverless platform.

list.text: Optimized: Each part is hosted where it performs best.
Scalable: Scale your API independently of your frontend.

box.content: Vitest & Elysia Handler Testing.
Fast, Vite-native unit testing for Svelte components and logic with full Rune support.
Isolated testing of Elysia routes by directly calling the `app.handle()` method with simulated requests.
box.code:
const res = await app.handle(new Request('http://localhost/'));
expect(await res.text()).toBe('Hello');

box.content: Ensuring that your tests reflect the same type safety as your production code via Eden.

list.text: Type-safe tests: Catch API breaks at compile time.
Reliability: Tests and code stay perfectly in sync.

box.content: Automating real user interactions across the entire stack to ensure seamless integration.
box.code:
await page.goto('/');
await expect(page.locator('h1')).toHaveText('Welcome');

box.content: The Future of Full-stack.
SvelteKit and Elysia represent a powerful, type-safe, and high-performance combination for modern web apps.

list.text: Speed: Bun-native performance everywhere.
Safety: End-to-end type safety with Eden.
Experience: Reactive UI with a minimalist API.

box.content: Keep an eye on Svelte 5 stable release, Elysia 1.0+ core improvements, and the growth of the Bun ecosystem.

list.text: Runes: The future of reactive programming.
Tooling: Faster builds and better DX with Vite.

box.content: Master the runes. Bridge the stack with Eden. Build the next generation of web apps.
Thank you! Any questions?
